secondary_archetype: AI tooling enablement / Design Ops PM

level_signals: Expected to build tools and agents independently in real time
Responsible for coaching, enabling, and systematically scaling practices across an org

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of experience in design thinking, product design, user experience, design engineering, or program management, and experience partnering with design teams

Demonstrated, hands-on experience building AI-powered tools, agents, automations, or workflows that transformed real work processes (not just using AI as a chatbot — building with it) for designers, researchers, creative teams, or product teams
Track record of coaching, teaching, or enabling others — formally or informally — with evidence that people you've helped actually changed how they work
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical audiences and adapt your style to different learners
Comfort working across multiple workstreams and relationships simultaneously (you'll be supporting Design teams at varying stages of their AI journey)
"Product builder" mindset to solve the "last mile" problem—transforming standalone AI experiments into polished, production-ready products that prioritize discoverability and ease of use

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience with design and research tooling ecosystems (Figma plugins, design system management, plugin APIs)
Proficiency with AI development tools and platforms (e.g., Claude, Claude Code, Codex, custom agent frameworks, API integrations, workflow automation tools)
Experience with change management, organizational transformation, or large-scale enablement programs
Experience building and scaling internal tools, templates, or playbooks that were adopted beyond your immediate team

scope.surface: AI tooling and workflow transformation for Stripe's Design organization (product designers, content designers, design researchers, design systems engineers, motion designers)
scope.business_model: internal platform / design ops — enabling function within a B2B fintech company
primary_metrics_inferred: Share of Design Stripes defaulting to AI as part of their work
Individual and cohort progress against a defined maturity model
core_responsibilities: Identify and document highest-leverage workflow transformations within Design's day-to-day work
Partner with designers to imagine AI-powered tools and agents that accelerate velocity
Build custom tools, agents, automations, and skills tailored to Design's workflows
Enable and uplevel designers to build and iterate on their own tools over time
Recognize patterns and systematically scale tools across the Design org
Document every tool, playbook, and transformation pattern
Share wins visibly across Design and with leadership
Track individual and cohort progress against a defined maturity model
Prepare designers for an agentic future including multi-agent workflows
Ensure tooling meets quality, security, and performance expectations
Source and evaluate vendors; interface with legal, security, and procurement

green_flags: Clearly articulated mission and success metrics (workflow transformation, maturity model progress)
High-impact, novel role at the intersection of AI and design — building a new operating model
Strong cross-functional exposure across design, engineering, research, product, and procurement
Emphasis on autonomy, action-bias, and building in real time alongside teams
red_flags: No location, compensation, or visa/sponsorship information disclosed
Role is explicitly on a new team building a new operating model — high ambiguity and potential lack of established support structures
Broad scope (brand, product, and marketing design) may lead to competing priorities

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 5+ years requirement and scope of responsibilities)

location.type (not stated in JD; left as 'unclear')

The role title includes 'Program Manager' but the responsibilities and persona description are heavily weighted toward hands-on AI building and coaching — archetype sits between Technical Program Manager and a design-focused AI enablement role. Classified as Technical Program Manager with secondary context added.
